{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Assistant Hygiene Manager",
  "description": "Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company&#39;s flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Assistant Hygiene Manager&nbsp;

Job&nbsp;Location: Apapa, Lagos

Purpose


	The Assistant Hygiene Manager will support the implementation and continuous improvement of hygiene, sanitation, and pest management programs across the milling operation.
	The role is responsible for ensuring compliance with food safety, quality, and regulatory standards while supporting the maintenance of a safe, hygienic, and efficient production environment.


The Job


	Support the implementation and monitoring of plant hygiene and housekeeping programs in line with food safety standards.
	Assist in managing Integrated Pest Management (IPM) and fumigation activities to ensure effective pest prevention and control.
	Support the management of product returns from trade through proper documentation, segregation, disposal, and preventive action implementation.
	Participate in internal and external audits and support the closure of identified gaps to ensure compliance with statutory and food safety requirements.


?Qualification?s


	HND / B.Sc Degree in Food Science or a related discipline.
	Professional hygiene certification will be an added advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of 3 years of relevant experience in an FMCG manufacturing environment.
	Certifications in Quality Management, Food Safety Management Systems, or Lean Six Sigma will be an added advantage.


The Person Must:


	Possess a good understanding of food safety, hygiene, and sanitation management systems.
	Have practical knowledge of Integrated Pest Management (IPM) and hygiene control programs.
	Demonstrate strong supervisory, organizational, and interpersonal skills.
	Be proactive with strong problem-solving and root cause analysis capabilities.
	Have good knowledge of GMP, 5S, Good Hygiene Practices (GHP), Good Housekeeping Practices (GHK), Integrated Pest Management (IPM), and Root Cause Analysis (RCA).

Assistant Hygiene Manager Job at Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc




        
    

        
        Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company's flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Assistant Hygiene Manager 

Job Location: Apapa, Lagos

Purpose
     

            
    




	The Assistant Hygiene Manager will support the implementation and continuous improvement of hygiene, sanitation, and pest management programs across the milling operation.
	The role is responsible for ensuring compliance with food safety, quality, and regulatory standards while supporting the maintenance of a safe, hygienic, and efficient production environment.


The Job


	Support the implementation and monitoring of plant hygiene and housekeeping programs in line with food safety standards.
	Assist in managing Integrated Pest Management (IPM) and fumigation activities to ensure effective pest prevention and control.
	Support the management of product returns from trade through proper documentation, segregation, disposal, and preventive action implementation.
	Participate in internal and external audits and support the closure of identified gaps to ensure compliance with statutory and food safety requirements.


?Qualification?s


	HND / B.Sc Degree in Food Science or a related discipline.
	Professional hygiene certification will be an added advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of 3 years of relevant experience in an FMCG manufacturing environment.
	Certifications in Quality Management, Food Safety Management Systems, or Lean Six Sigma will be an added advantage.


The Person Must:


	Possess a good understanding of food safety, hygiene, and sanitation management systems.
	Have practical knowledge of Integrated Pest Management (IPM) and hygiene control programs.
	Demonstrate strong supervisory, organizational, and interpersonal skills.
	Be proactive with strong problem-solving and root cause analysis capabilities.
	Have good knowledge of GMP, 5S, Good Hygiene Practices (GHP), Good Housekeeping Practices (GHK), Integrated Pest Management (IPM), and Root Cause Analysis (RCA).
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "MEP QA/QC Engineer (Junior Engineer)",
  "description": "Ascentech Services Limited is the most sought-after Human Resource Solution Provider with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We have a reputation for satisfying and surpassing clients expectations through our core offerings: Recruitment and Selection, Outsourcing, Training and Development. To accomplish these we leverage on creating and managing change in organizations by improving efficiency through our proven and dynamic HR solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: MEP QA/QC Engineer (Junior Engineer)

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Our client is seeking a detail-oriented MEP QA/QC Engineer to oversee the quality assurance and quality control of Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing (MEP) systems on construction projects.
	The successful candidate will ensure all installations comply with project specifications, international standards, safety regulations, and client requirements while supporting testing, commissioning, and project handover activities.


Key Responsibilities


	Implement QA/QC procedures for MEP works in line with project specifications, approved drawings, and international standards.
	Conduct inspections, testing, and quality audits of MEP installations, materials, and workmanship.
	Prepare and maintain QA/QC documentation, including inspection reports, ITPs, checklists, and non-conformance reports (NCRs).
	Monitor compliance with health, safety, environmental, and quality standards throughout project execution.
	Coordinate with contractors, consultants, suppliers, and third-party inspectors to ensure quality compliance.
	Support testing, commissioning, defect resolution, and final handover of MEP systems.


Requirements


	HND or Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in MEP QA/QC within the construction industry.
	Strong knowledge of MEP systems, QA/QC processes, construction standards, and international codes (ASHRAE, NFPA, IEC, etc.).
	Proficiency in AutoCAD, BIM, Microsoft Office, and quality management systems is an added advantage.
	Excellent analytical, communication, problem-solving, and documentation skills.


Salary
N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: cv@ascentech.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

MEP QA/QC Engineer (Junior Engineer) Job at Ascentech Services Limited




        
    

        
        Ascentech Services Limited is the most sought-after Human Resource Solution Provider with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We have a reputation for satisfying and surpassing clients expectations through our core offerings: Recruitment and Selection, Outsourcing, Training and Development. To accomplish these we leverage on creating and managing change in organizations by improving efficiency through our proven and dynamic HR solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: MEP QA/QC Engineer (Junior Engineer)

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	Our client is seeking a detail-oriented MEP QA/QC Engineer to oversee the quality assurance and quality control of Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing (MEP) systems on construction projects.
	The successful candidate will ensure all installations comply with project specifications, international standards, safety regulations, and client requirements while supporting testing, commissioning, and project handover activities.


Key Responsibilities


	Implement QA/QC procedures for MEP works in line with project specifications, approved drawings, and international standards.
	Conduct inspections, testing, and quality audits of MEP installations, materials, and workmanship.
	Prepare and maintain QA/QC documentation, including inspection reports, ITPs, checklists, and non-conformance reports (NCRs).
	Monitor compliance with health, safety, environmental, and quality standards throughout project execution.
	Coordinate with contractors, consultants, suppliers, and third-party inspectors to ensure quality compliance.
	Support testing, commissioning, defect resolution, and final handover of MEP systems.


Requirements


	HND or Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in MEP QA/QC within the construction industry.
	Strong knowledge of MEP systems, QA/QC processes, construction standards, and international codes (ASHRAE, NFPA, IEC, etc.).
	Proficiency in AutoCAD, BIM, Microsoft Office, and quality management systems is an added advantage.
	Excellent analytical, communication, problem-solving, and documentation skills.


Salary
N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Full Stack Developer",
  "description": "Walexbiz Group, a dynamic conglomerate dedicated to pushing the boundaries of industry. We are a reverence to the power of synergy and collaboration, with a diverse array of subsidiaries. Each of our subsidiaries is an example of industry greatness, driven by a shared dedication to innovation, integrity, and impact.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Full Stack Developer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a skilled and motivated Full Stack Developer to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing, developing, testing, and maintaining web applications across both the front-end and back-end.
	You will work closely with product managers, designers, and other developers to deliver scalable, secure, and user-friendly software solutions.


Key Responsibilities


	Design, develop, and maintain responsive web applications.
	Build intuitive user interfaces using modern front-end technologies.
	Develop and maintain server-side logic, APIs, and databases.
	Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define and implement new features.
	Optimize applications for speed, scalability, and security.
	Troubleshoot, debug, and resolve software issues.
	Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code.
	Conduct code reviews and ensure adherence to coding standards.
	Integrate third-party services and APIs where required.
	Participate in software testing, deployment, and maintenance activities.
	Stay current with emerging technologies and recommend improvements.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Timely delivery of assigned development tasks.
	Code quality and adherence to best practices.
	System uptime and application performance.
	Bug resolution turnaround time.
	Successful deployment of new features.
	Team collaboration and contribution to technical improvements.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).
	3&ndash;5 years of experience in full stack web development.
	Strong proficiency in HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and TypeScript.
	Experience with modern front-end frameworks such as React, Angular, or Vue.js.
	Experience with back-end technologies such as Node.js, Python (Django/FastAPI), PHP (Laravel), Java (Spring Boot), or .NET.
	Strong knowledge of RESTful APIs and web services.
	Experience working with relational databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server) and/or NoSQL databases (MongoDB).
	Familiarity with Git and version control systems.
	Understanding of software development methodologies such as Agile/Scrum.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud Platform.
	Knowledge of Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.
	Experience with microservices architecture.
	Familiarity with automated testing frameworks.
	Knowledge of DevOps practices is an added advantage.


Technical Skills:


	HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, TypeScript
	React, Angular, or Vue.js
	Node.js, Express.js, Laravel, Django, Spring Boot, or ASP.NET Core
	REST APIs and GraphQL
	MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB
	Git/GitHub/GitLab
	Docker and CI/CD
	Linux server basics
	Cloud services (AWS, Azure, or GCP)


Soft Skills:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
	Strong attention to detail.
	Effective time management and organizational skills.
	Willingness to learn and adapt to new technologies.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary.
	Professional development and training opportunities.
	Collaborative and innovative work environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV, portfolio (if available), and links to GitHub or other relevant projects for consideration to: hrm@walexbizgroup.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Full Stack Developer Job at Walex Biz Group




        
    

        
        Walexbiz Group, a dynamic conglomerate dedicated to pushing the boundaries of industry. We are a reverence to the power of synergy and collaboration, with a diverse array of subsidiaries. Each of our subsidiaries is an example of industry greatness, driven by a shared dedication to innovation, integrity, and impact.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Full Stack Developer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a skilled and motivated Full Stack Developer to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing, developing, testing, and maintaining web applications across both the front-end and back-end.
	You will work closely with product managers, designers, and other developers to deliver scalable, secure, and user-friendly software solutions.


Key Responsibilities


	Design, develop, and maintain responsive web applications.
	Build intuitive user interfaces using modern front-end technologies.
	Develop and maintain server-side logic, APIs, and databases.
	Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define and implement new features.
	Optimize applications for speed, scalability, and security.
	Troubleshoot, debug, and resolve software issues.
	Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code.
	Conduct code reviews and ensure adherence to coding standards.
	Integrate third-party services and APIs where required.
	Participate in software testing, deployment, and maintenance activities.
	Stay current with emerging technologies and recommend improvements.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Timely delivery of assigned development tasks.
	Code quality and adherence to best practices.
	System uptime and application performance.
	Bug resolution turnaround time.
	Successful deployment of new features.
	Team collaboration and contribution to technical improvements.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).
	3–5 years of experience in full stack web development.
	Strong proficiency in HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and TypeScript.
	Experience with modern front-end frameworks such as React, Angular, or Vue.js.
	Experience with back-end technologies such as Node.js, Python (Django/FastAPI), PHP (Laravel), Java (Spring Boot), or .NET.
	Strong knowledge of RESTful APIs and web services.
	Experience working with relational databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server) and/or NoSQL databases (MongoDB).
	Familiarity with Git and version control systems.
	Understanding of software development methodologies such as Agile/Scrum.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud Platform.
	Knowledge of Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.
	Experience with microservices architecture.
	Familiarity with automated testing frameworks.
	Knowledge of DevOps practices is an added advantage.


Technical Skills:


	HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, TypeScript
	React, Angular, or Vue.js
	Node.js, Express.js, Laravel, Django, Spring Boot, or ASP.NET Core
	REST APIs and GraphQL
	MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB
	Git/GitHub/GitLab
	Docker and CI/CD
	Linux server basics
	Cloud services (AWS, Azure, or GCP)


Soft Skills:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

	Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
	Strong attention to detail.
	Effective time management and organizational skills.
	Willingness to learn and adapt to new technologies.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary.

	Collaborative and innovative work environment.

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Health, Safety and Environment Supervisor",
  "description": "Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company&#39;s flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Health, Safety and Environment Supervisor, Northern Nigeria

Job Location: Kano

Purpose


	The Health, Safety &amp; Environment (HSE) Supervisor will support the implementation and continuous improvement of health, safety, and environmental standards across the facility. The role is responsible for promoting a strong safety culture, ensuring compliance with regulatory and company requirements, and preventing workplace accidents and injuries.


The Job


	Conduct routine inspections to identify hazards, unsafe acts, and unsafe working conditions.
	Promote safety awareness through training, education, and employee engagement initiatives.
	Manage first aid facilities and ensure prompt response to workplace injuries and incidents.
	Investigate and report accidents, incidents, and near misses, while driving corrective actions.
	Support the development, review, and implementation of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).
	Participate in safety audits, safety observations, and workplace risk assessments.
	Maintain accurate HSE records, reports, and documentation.
	Drive compliance with HSE regulations, corporate safety policies, FSSC requirements, HACCP, EIA, and OSHA standards.


?Qualification?s


	5 O&rsquo; level Credits including English &amp; Mathematics in not more than two sittings.
	OND qualification in a related field. in Environmental Sciences, Engineering or a related discipline.


Experience:


	Minimum of two (2) years of cognate experience in Health, Safety &amp; Environment management.
	Experience within a manufacturing environment is highly preferred.


The Person Must:


	Have a strong understanding of workplace health, safety, and environmental management principles.
	Demonstrate excellent hazard identification and risk assessment skills.
	Possess strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to influence safe behaviors.

Health, Safety and Environment Supervisor Job at Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc




        
    

        
        Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company's flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Health, Safety and Environment Supervisor, Northern Nigeria

Job Location: Kano

Purpose
     

            
    




	The Health, Safety & Environment (HSE) Supervisor will support the implementation and continuous improvement of health, safety, and environmental standards across the facility. The role is responsible for promoting a strong safety culture, ensuring compliance with regulatory and company requirements, and preventing workplace accidents and injuries.


The Job


	Conduct routine inspections to identify hazards, unsafe acts, and unsafe working conditions.
	Promote safety awareness through training, education, and employee engagement initiatives.
	Manage first aid facilities and ensure prompt response to workplace injuries and incidents.
	Investigate and report accidents, incidents, and near misses, while driving corrective actions.
	Support the development, review, and implementation of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).
	Participate in safety audits, safety observations, and workplace risk assessments.
	Maintain accurate HSE records, reports, and documentation.
	Drive compliance with HSE regulations, corporate safety policies, FSSC requirements, HACCP, EIA, and OSHA standards.


?Qualification?s


	5 O’ level Credits including English & Mathematics in not more than two sittings.
	OND qualification in a related field. in Environmental Sciences, Engineering or a related discipline.


Experience:


	Minimum of two (2) years of cognate experience in Health, Safety & Environment management.
	Experience within a manufacturing environment is highly preferred.


The Person Must:


	Have a strong understanding of workplace health, safety, and environmental management principles.
	Demonstrate excellent hazard identification and risk assessment skills.
	Possess strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to influence safe behaviors.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

As part of our continued expansion, we are seeking passionate, innovative, and results-driven professionals to fill the position below:

Job Position: Aftersales Technical Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Reports To: Head, Business Development &amp; Sales
Slots: 2&nbsp;

Job Summary


	The Aftersales Technical Engineer will be responsible for providing post-installation technical support, preventive and corrective maintenance, troubleshooting, commissioning, and customer training to ensure the optimal performance of the Company&#39;s renewable energy systems.
	The successful candidate will play a key role in delivering exceptional customer experience while ensuring maximum system uptime.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide technical support to customers on installed solar PV and hybrid energy systems.
	Carry out preventive and corrective maintenance on solar PV systems, batteries, inverters, and associated electrical equipment.
	Diagnose and resolve technical faults using appropriate diagnostic tools.

	Conduct routine site inspections and system performance assessments.
	Prepare comprehensive service reports after every maintenance visit.
	Train customers on the safe operation and maintenance of installed systems.
	Maintain accurate service records, spare parts inventory, and customer service history.
	Escalate complex technical issues and recommend long-term corrective actions.
	Ensure strict compliance with Health, Safety, Environment (HSE), and electrical safety standards.
	Participate in continuous technical training and product knowledge development.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Renewable Energy, Mechatronics, or a related discipline.
	2 - 4 years of practical experience in solar PV installation, commissioning, maintenance, and troubleshooting.
	Good knowledge of Solar PV Systems, Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS), hybrid energy systems, inverters, and charge controllers.
	Ability to interpret electrical drawings and technical documentation.
	Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent communication and customer service skills.
	Valid driver&#39;s licence and willingness to travel to project locations.

	Competitive salary
	Attractive performance-based incentives
	Health Insurance (HMO)
	Pension Scheme
	Continuous learning and professional development opportunities
	Career growth within a rapidly expanding renewable energy company
	Supportive and collaborative work environment
	Official vehicle or transportation support (role/performance dependent)

Aftersales Technical Engineer Job at Virtutis Solaris Limited




        
    

        
        Virtutis Solaris Limited, a sister company of Mojec International Limited, is a leading renewable energy solutions provider committed to delivering clean, reliable, cost-effective, and sustainable electricity across Nigeria. We empower homes, businesses, and communities through innovative energy solutions, including Solar Home Systems (SHS), Mini-Grids, Commercial & Industrial (C&I) Solar Solutions, and Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS).

As part of our continued expansion, we are seeking passionate, innovative, and results-driven professionals to fill the position below:

Job Position: Aftersales Technical Engineer
     

            
    



Job Location: Lagos
Reports To: Head, Business Development & Sales
Slots: 2 

Job Summary


	The Aftersales Technical Engineer will be responsible for providing post-installation technical support, preventive and corrective maintenance, troubleshooting, commissioning, and customer training to ensure the optimal performance of the Company's renewable energy systems.
	The successful candidate will play a key role in delivering exceptional customer experience while ensuring maximum system uptime.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide technical support to customers on installed solar PV and hybrid energy systems.
	Carry out preventive and corrective maintenance on solar PV systems, batteries, inverters, and associated electrical equipment.
	Diagnose and resolve technical faults using appropriate diagnostic tools.
	Commission newly installed renewable energy systems and verify operational performance.
	Conduct routine site inspections and system performance assessments.
	Prepare comprehensive service reports after every maintenance visit.
	Train customers on the safe operation and maintenance of installed systems.
	Maintain accurate service records, spare parts inventory, and customer service history.
	Escalate complex technical issues and recommend long-term corrective actions.
	Ensure strict compliance with Health, Safety, Environment (HSE), and electrical safety standards.
	Participate in continuous technical training and product knowledge development.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Renewable Energy, Mechatronics, or a related discipline.
	2 - 4 years of practical experience in solar PV installation, commissioning, maintenance, and troubleshooting.
	Good knowledge of Solar PV Systems, Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS), hybrid energy systems, inverters, and charge controllers.
	Ability to interpret electrical drawings and technical documentation.
	Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent communication and customer service skills.
	Valid driver's licence and willingness to travel to project locations.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary
	Attractive performance-based incentives
	Health Insurance (HMO)
	Pension Scheme
	Continuous learning and professional development opportunities
	Career growth within a rapidly expanding renewable energy company
	Supportive and collaborative work environment
	Official vehicle or transportation support (role/performance dependent)


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application  to: [email protected] and copy [email protected] using the job title as the subject of the email.

Note: Candidates should include ther current renumeration in the email body.
